Caption: The concept of using sensors for detection and early warning of battery thermal runaway.

Scanning electron microscopy (SEM) images of the Al2O3/ZnO heterostructured layers after thermal annealing at 600 °C for 30 min containing a nano-layer of Al2O3 with a thickness of: (a) 7 nm; (b) 10 nm; and (c) 12 nm.
(a) X-ray diffraction (XRD) pattern; and (b) micro-Raman spectrum of the 10 nm-Al2O3/ZnO heterostructured layers thermally annealed (TA)- treated at 600 °C for 30 min.
Top and bottom view of the investigated sensor structure based on Al2O3/ZnO.

(a) Gas response versus different thickness of 5, 7, 10, 12, 15 nm of the Al2O3 overlayer. (b) Gas response versus operating temperatures of the Al2O3/ZnO sample set containing the 10 nm overlayer of Al2O3 after thermally annealing (TA) at 600 °C for 30 min.

(a) Response to different concentrations of C3H6O2 of the Al2O3/ZnO samples set with a thickness of 10 nm of the Al2O3 overlayer thermally annealled (TA) at 600 °C for 30 min (b) Dynamic response to concentrations of 5, 10 and 100 ppm of the Al2O3/ZnO sample set with a thickness of 10 nm of the Al2O3 overlayer thermally annealed (TA) at 600 °C for 30 min (c) Variation of the gas response to 5 ppm of C3H6O2 vapors over time for the sample containing the 10 nm overlayer of Al2O3.

Top and bottom panels represent the side and top projections of the optimised structures of (a) α-Al2O3(0001) surface, (b) ZnO 10 1 ̅ 0 surface and (c) Al2O3/ZnO 10 1 ̅ 0 interface. The stacking of the atomic layers are specified. The exposed planes containing ions with dangling bonds are emphasized in the top projection. O atoms are in red, Zn atoms are in grey and Al atoms are in blue.

Simulated scanning tunnelling microscopy (STM) profiles of (a) α-Al2O3(0001) facet, (b) ZnO 10 1 ̅ 0 facet and (c) Al2O3/ZnO 10 1 ̅ 0 interface. The bias used was (a) V = −2.5 eV, (b) V = −2.0 eV and (c) V = −2.0 eV; the density used was (a) ρ = 0.0036 e Å−3, (b) ρ = 0.0040 e Å−3 and (c) ρ = 0.0010 e Å−3 and the tip distance used was (a) d = 1.50 Å, (b) d = 2.00 Å and (c) d = 2.40 Å. O atoms are in red, Zn atoms are in grey and Al atoms are in blue.

Adsorption of (a) C3H6O2, (b) C4H10O2, (c) NO2, (d) PF5 and (e) H2O on the Zn site of the Al2O3/ZnO( 10 1 ̅ 0 ) surface. Interatomic distances are indicated. O atoms are in red, H atoms are in white, C atoms are in brown, F atoms are in light blue, P atoms are in pink, Zn atoms are in grey and Al atoms are in blue.
Columnar Al2O3/ZnO films show high sensitivity and selectivity towards the components of the electrolyte of Li-ion batteries with negligible impact of humidity for battery safety applications.
